big_picture: Today's top Indian policy news centers on energy and economic disruptions. PM Modi accused Congress of "spreading panic amid war crisis," asserting the government is working to shield citizens from global conflicts and economic disruptions. The Reserve Bank faces pressure to halt interest rate cuts due to inflation risks from the West Asia crisis and fading base effects. Meanwhile, LPG shortages in Kerala have driven demand for firewood, with hotels and eateries turning to alternative fuels. Since February 28, when Israel and the USA attacked Iran, thousands of Indians in West Asia remain stranded. Additionally, the Centre revoked Sonam Wangchuk’s detention under the National Security Act after over five months. The USA targeted military sites on Iran's Kharg Island, threatening its oil infrastructure amid rising tensions.

Meanwhile, economists suggest India's Reserve Bank may halt interest rate cuts due to inflation risks from the West Asia crisis.

Infrastructure.top_article_title: PM arrives in Kolkata, to unveil development projects worth over Rs 18,500 cr

Energy.summary: The Indian government is exploring alternatives to flexible gas plants due to uncertainties in West Asia, while the Confederation of All India Traders urged Madhya Pradesh to restore limited LPG supplies to prevent shutdowns of hotels and eateries. Meanwhile, petrol and diesel prices in India remained unchanged on March 14 despite global oil market volatility.
Energy.top_article_title: West Asia conflict | With question mark on flexible gas plants, govt explores alternatives

Governance.summary: Uttar Pradesh's Civil Supplies Department conducted raids at 1,483 locations to check black marketing of LPG cylinders, resulting in six arrests, while India's new Income Tax Act, 2025 reorganizes provisions related to return processing, scrutiny assessments, and reassessment of escaped income.
Governance.top_article_title: Lucknow : Raids To Check Black Marketing Of LPG Cylinders

Welfare.summary: The Indian government's minority scholarship scheme has been frozen for three years amid a fraud investigation, with a standing committee highlighting the resulting "injustice to minority students," while the minority affairs ministry plans to send a study team to states to assess the situation. Meanwhile, a case has been filed against a school in Attur Layout after children were allegedly forced to stand in the blazing sun, and Udhayanidhi Stalin is set to inaugurate a lounge for construction workers and a village knowledge center in Tirupattur.
Welfare.top_article_title: Govt’s minority scholarship scheme frozen 3 yrs as probe into fraud meanders, students pay price

Trade.summary: India's Commerce Minister Piyush Goyal rejected reports that India is holding off a US trade deal, emphasizing the long-term benefits in technology and investment, while the Trump administration launched a trade investigation into India under Section 301 of the US Trade Act.
Trade.top_article_title: Global sugar prices fall amid surplus supply from Brazil: ICRA

Finance.summary: The Indian Cabinet approved four railway and road projects worth Rs 11,944 crore, while the IFSCA proposed regulations for Managing General Agents to strengthen the IFSC insurance framework. Additionally, the Reserve Bank issued directions under the Banking Regulation Act against Kanaka Pattana Sahakara Bank Niyamita in Davangere.
Finance.top_article_title: Cabinet approves 4 railways, road projects worth Rs 11,944 crore

Technology.summary: The Indian consulate in Dubai has set up a 24x7 control room to assist Indians amid the Middle East conflict, while Y Combinator has launched its free online Startup School in India to support early-stage entrepreneurs. Meanwhile, longevity startup Foxo aims to empower individuals to take charge of their health.
Technology.top_article_title: How Longevity Startup Foxo Wants To Turn Individuals Into “CEOs Of Their Health”

Education.summary: State School Education Minister Dada Bhuse warned CBSE, ICSE, and international schools to comply with the Marathi language rule, while the NCTE urged the inclusion of the school education department in a key panel. Meanwhile, a lecturer in Kangra was suspended, and an HP board exam center was shifted after a video allegedly showed mass copying through a microphone.
Education.top_article_title: Mass copying through a microphone? Video leads to lecturer’s suspension and shifting of HP board exam centre in Kangra

Health.summary: The Health Minister launched "Mission Safe Pregnancy," a screening drive aimed at improving early detection and maternal health outcomes, while Sebi Chairman Tuhin Kanta Pandey addressed market volatility caused by the Iran-Israel conflict, urging patience. Kerala's success in reducing infant mortality highlights its effective healthcare interventions.
Health.top_article_title: A cradle of care: How Kerala fights infant mortality

Environment.summary: Several Maharashtra cities recorded temperatures above 40°C, with rain and thunderstorms predicted from March 17, while international weather models suggest El Niño may emerge post-July, potentially affecting India's monsoon and agriculture. Additionally, efforts to protect and revive India’s rivers were highlighted on the International Day of Action for Rivers, honoring environmental leader R. Nallakannu of Tamil Nadu.
Environment.top_article_title: Several Maharashtra Cities Record Temperatures Above 40°C; Rain and Thunderstorms Predicted from March 17

Agriculture.summary: A parliamentary panel urged the finance ministry to allocate fertilizer subsidy funds at the budget estimate stage to align with actual needs, while Maharashtra is positioning cashew as an anchor crop for natural farming to boost farmer incomes and ecological resilience. Meanwhile, biofertilizers are increasingly influencing farming practices in India, shaping the future of agriculture.
Agriculture.top_article_title: Parliamentary panel seeks allocation of realistic fertiliser subsidy fund at budget estimate stage

Fintech.summary: A panel has called for making the Unified Payments Interface (UPI) more viable and enhancing cybersecurity measures in India.

Urban.summary: The Income Tax Appellate Tribunal (ITAT) Delhi ruled that gifting property to a wife, especially when she is a co-sharer, does not constitute a colourable device to claim exemption under Section 54F of the Income Tax Act. Meanwhile, Maharashtra has paused new permits for rickshaws and taxis, requiring e-rickshaws and e-bikes to obtain licences to improve urban mobility regulation and safety. Additionally, a report revealed that the retention rate in Mumbai’s municipal schools dropped below 50%, with a decline in student transitions from Class 8 to Class 9.
Urban.top_article_title: Gift of property to wife not colourable device to taint claim of exemption u/s. 54F

articles.title: Supreme Court Stays Defamation Case Against Lawyer For Posting Book Extracts On Mamata Banerjee's Personal Life
articles.summary: The Supreme Court has stayed a defamation case against a lawyer who posted extracts from a book discussing the personal life of West Bengal Chief Minister Mamata Banerjee. The court issued a notice and ordered a status quo in the case, which was filed to quash a defamation complaint. This ruling highlights the balance between free speech and legal accountability in India.

articles.title: Centre revokes Sonam Wangchuk’s detention under National Security Act
articles.summary: The Indian government has revoked the detention of Sonam Wangchuk under the National Security Act (NSA). This action follows legal or administrative review, highlighting concerns over the use of stringent security laws and their impact on civil liberties. The decision underscores ongoing debates about the balance between national security and individual freedoms in India.
articles.context_summary: Sonam Wangchuk was detained on September 26, 2025 after violent protests rocked Leh amid demands for statehood for Ladakh as well as its inclusion under the Sixth Schedule of the Constitution.

articles.title: LTCG Cannot Be Taxed in Wife’s Hands When Property Settled by Husband – ITAT Deletes Rs. 19.86 Cr Addition
articles.summary: The Income Tax Appellate Tribunal (ITAT) ruled that long-term capital gains (LTCG) from property transferred to a spouse without consideration must be taxed in the hands of the transferor (husband) under Section 64(1)(iv). The tribunal invalidated the tax assessment in the wife's hands, deleting a Rs. 19.86 crore addition. This ruling clarifies tax laws related to inter-spousal property transfers and has implications for tax planning and compliance.
articles.raw_summary: The Tribunal held that capital gains from property transferred to a spouse without consideration must be taxed in the hands of the transferor under Section 64(1)(iv). Assessing it again in the transferee spouse’s hands was invalid. The post LTCG Cannot Be Taxed in Wife’s Hands When Property Settled by Husband – ITAT Deletes Rs. 19.86 Cr Addition appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: Entire Bank Deposits Cannot Be Treated as Income – ITAT Directs 8% Profit Estimation
articles.summary: The Income Tax Appellate Tribunal (ITAT) ruled that bank deposits linked to business activity cannot be fully taxed as unexplained income under Section 69. Instead, it directed an 8% profit estimation for such deposits. This decision clarifies tax treatment for businesses and individuals, ensuring fair taxation while preventing overreach by tax authorities.
articles.raw_summary: The issue was whether the entire bank deposits could be treated as unexplained income under Section 69. The tribunal held that deposits linked to business activity cannot be fully taxed and directed estimation of profit at 8%. The post Entire Bank Deposits Cannot Be Treated as Income   ITAT Directs 8% Profit Estimation appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: West Asia crisis: The Indians caught in the crossfire
articles.summary: Since February 28, when Israel and the U.S. jointly attacked Iran, plunging the entire West Asian region into war, thousands of Indian tourists and expatriates have been left stranded across the Gulf. Indian missions have struggled to keep up with requests and demands. From missile and drone strikes, to soaring prices, to communication blackouts, the conflict has left civilians vulnerable, reports Kallol Bhattacherjee

articles.title: Reopening Notice Invalid Without Proper Section u/s 151 – ITAT Quashes Reassessment
articles.summary: The Income Tax Appellate Tribunal (ITAT) ruled that a reassessment notice issued beyond three years was invalid because it lacked proper approval from higher authorities under Section 151(ii) of the Income Tax Act. The tribunal quashed the notice since the approval was obtained only from the Principal Chief Commissioner of Income Tax (PCIT) instead of the required higher authority. This decision reinforces procedural safeguards in tax reassessment cases.
articles.raw_summary: The Tribunal held that reopening beyond three years requires sanction from higher authorities under Section 151(ii). Since approval was obtained only from the PCIT, the reassessment notice was declared invalid. The post Reopening Notice Invalid Without Proper Section u/s 151 – ITAT Quashes Reassessment appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: Attempt To Settle Dispute Cannot Prevent Police From Taking Cognizance Of Crime : Supreme Court
articles.summary: The Supreme Court ruled that police reconciliation efforts do not prevent them from registering FIRs for criminal acts. The case involved a dispute between two groups in Punjab, with the court emphasizing that criminal acts must be addressed regardless of reconciliation attempts. The ruling reinforces the police's duty to uphold the law.
articles.raw_summary: The Supreme Court has observed that an attempt by the police to reconcile a dispute between the clashing groups would not bar them from registering an FIR for criminal acts. “The mere attempt at reconciliation cannot prevent the police from taking cognizance of criminal acts.” , observed a bench of Justices Sanjay Kumar and K. articles.title: 'Privy Purse Privileges To Princely Rulers Can't Be Claimed As Legal Rights' : Supreme Court Rejects Mizo Chiefs' Claim
articles.summary: The Supreme Court rejected the claim by Mizo Chiefs that they were entitled to privy purse benefits, ruling that such privileges cannot be claimed as legal rights. The decision clarifies the constitutional position on privy purses, which were abolished in 1971. The ruling has implications for similar claims by other princely rulers.
articles.raw_summary: The Court rejected the argument of Mizo Chiefs that they were at par with the other princely rulers and hence entitled to privy purse benefits.

articles.title: Privy purse not a right: Supreme Court rejects compensation claim by Mizo chiefs for land taken in 1955
articles.summary: The Supreme Court rejected a compensation claim by Mizo chiefs for land taken by the government in 1955, ruling that the privy purse is not a right. The decision clarifies legal precedents on land acquisition and compensation, impacting similar cases.

articles.title: Sonam Wangchuk’s detention under NSA revoked by Centre after over five months

Wangchuk was detained in September 2023 following protests in Leh demanding statehood for Ladakh and its inclusion in the Sixth Schedule of the Constitution. The revocation highlights the government's use of security laws against activists and the broader debate on civil liberties in India.

articles.title: Ladakh climate activist Sonam Wangchuk to be released from jail as govt revokes NSA detention
articles.summary: The Indian government has revoked the detention of climate activist Sonam Wangchuk under the National Security Act (NSA) with immediate effect. The revocation highlights the government's use of stringent security laws and the judicial scrutiny they face.

articles.title: How persistently high oil prices amid Iran war could impact India's economy
articles.summary: Escalating oil prices due to the Iran war pose a significant threat to India's economy. With nearly 90% of crude imported, sustained high oil costs could widen the current account deficit, weaken the rupee, and fuel inflation. Government finances may also face pressure from increased subsidies, potentially impacting infrastructure spending and economic growth.

articles.summary: Indian housing sales saw a slight dip in units last year but a significant rise in value. Demand for homes above Rs 1 crore drove this growth. articles.title: Stay patient, volatility temporary, says Sebi Chairman as Iran-Israel war ruffles global markets
articles.summary: Sebi Chairman Tuhin Kanta Pandey reassured investors that market volatility, fueled by geopolitical tensions like the Iran-Israel/US conflict, is temporary. He emphasized that the true test of financial markets lies in their ability to function smoothly, fairly, and efficiently despite uncertainty, drawing parallels to past disruptions like the COVID-19 pandemic and the Russia-Ukraine war.

articles.title: AI cannot replace trained mind of lawyer, ethical responsibility of judge: Justice Vikram Nath
articles.summary: Justice Vikram Nath of the Supreme Court emphasized that AI cannot replace the trained mind of a lawyer or the ethical responsibility of a judge. He stated that while technology may assist in drafting notes, it should not be allowed to invent laws. He also warned against the misuse of AI leading to an extreme rejection of its use in the judicial system.

articles.summary: Iran is using drones and missiles to disrupt oil flows through the Strait of Hormuz, aiming to pressure the US and inflict economic damage. This move follows a separate probe into excess industrial capacity.

The government also reduced its total expenditure projection for the year.

articles.title: RBI unlikely to hike rates now despite crude inflation as it is supply-driven shock, not demand: Natixis Economist
articles.summary: The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) is unlikely to immediately raise interest rates despite inflationary pressures from rising crude oil prices, as the current situation reflects a supply-side shock rather than demand-driven inflation, according to Trinh Nguyen, Senior economist, Emerging Asia at Natixis.

articles.title: West Asia conflict poses downside risk, India GDP growth seen at 7.1 pc in FY27: Crisil Intelligence
articles.summary: India's economy faces risks from West Asia conflict affecting oil prices. Real GDP growth is expected at 7.1 percent in FY27. The Reserve Bank of India is likely to maintain interest rates.

articles.summary: India has clarified key aspects of the Press Note 3 framework governing investments from land-bordering countries, introducing a 10% beneficial ownership threshold and time-bound approvals for select sectors. articles.summary: Indian markets mirrored global trends as US stocks closed lower. Volatile crude oil prices, influenced by the Iran conflict, impacted equities. articles.summary: The Indian government has decided to revoke the detention of climate activist Sonam Wangchuk, who was held under the National Security Act (NSA) following violent protests in Leh last September. Wangchuk was lodged in Jodhpur jail in Rajasthan. The decision comes months after his arrest and highlights the use of stringent laws like the NSA in handling protests and dissent.
articles.raw_summary: Climate activist Sonam Wangchuk was detained and lodged in Rajasthan's Jodhpur jail under the NSA following violent Leh protests last September.

articles.summary: Iran also criticised European countries for backing an "illegal war" against Iran, claiming they expected American support against Russia in return.

articles.title: Planning a trip this summer? How rising jet fuel prices could push up flight fares
articles.summary: Rising jet fuel prices may lead to higher flight fares, particularly on long-haul international routes. This could impact travel costs for passengers and may prompt policy discussions on fuel pricing or aviation sector regulations in India.
articles.raw_summary: The impact may be felt most on long-haul international routes, which burn significantly more fuel than shorter flights.

articles.summary: The Supreme Court has stayed a defamation case against lawyer Koustav Bagchi, who shared an excerpt from a book about West Bengal Chief Minister Mamata Banerjee's private life on Facebook. The ruling could impact free speech and legal precedents in India. The case highlights tensions between privacy rights and public discourse.

articles.title: India’s Nobel Prize paradox: A long gap for laureates and why Mahatma Gandhi remains the most famous non-winner
articles.summary: Despite India's rich intellectual tradition and early Nobel laureates like Tagore and Raman, the country has experienced long gaps in recognition. Factors like brain drain, limited funding, and an emphasis on publications over long-term innovation hinder the journey from research to global acclaim. The case of Mahatma Gandhi's missed Peace Prize also highlights complexities beyond merit.

The case of Mahatma Gandhi's missed Peace Prize also highlights complexities beyond merit.

articles.title: Govt tables bill proposing more precise definition of transgender, drops ‘self-perceived gender identity’
articles.summary: The Indian government has introduced a bill that proposes a more precise definition of transgender, removing the concept of 'self-perceived gender identity.' This change could impact legal recognition and rights for transgender individuals in India.

articles.title: Oppn files notices in both Houses for removal of CEC
articles.summary: The opposition has filed notices in both Houses of Parliament seeking the removal of the Chief Election Commissioner (CEC). This move highlights political tensions but does not directly involve policy changes or civil liberties issues.

This decision impacts universities and students by clarifying the tax treatment of such fees.

Goa's digital infrastructure and educational institutions will support this initiative.

articles.title: Maharashtra's agri AI policy will revolutionise farm sector: Minister Dattatreya Bharane
articles.summary: Maharashtra's Minister Dattatreya Bharane announced the state's new Maha Agri-AI policy, which aims to revolutionize the farm sector by leveraging artificial intelligence and other advanced technologies. The policy includes initiatives like the 'MahaVISTAAR - AI' app, crop insurance reforms, and mechanization efforts to boost farm productivity. This is a first-of-its-kind policy in India, highlighting the state's focus on technological interventions in agriculture.

articles.raw_summary: The Supreme Court denied a plea for mandatory NAT in blood banks, advising Sarvesham Mangalam Foundation to consult health departments, stressing reliance on domain experts.

articles.title: Supreme Court to use AI to decide case listing, bench allocation

articles.raw_summary: The new system will minimise the role of officials in the court registry who currently handle the scheduling of cases and the assignment of matters to different benches of judges.

articles.title: Bombay HC issues notice to Centre on plea over alleged LPG export amid domestic shortage
articles.summary: The Bombay High Court has issued a notice to the Central government over a petition concerning LPG exports during a domestic shortage. This highlights concerns over energy security and trade regulations.
articles.raw_summary: Pending the final decision on the petition, the court directed CPIL to ensure that the storage and supply of LPG meant for domestic consumption strictly follow prevailing policy of the Central government. The matter has been scheduled for further hearing on March 17.

articles.raw_summary: Chaturvedi voices concern about what she calls ‘judicial overreach’ in SC’s direction to Centre, states to ‘dissociate’ from academics, who drafted the judiciary chapter.

articles.title: Supreme Court says paid menstrual leave will end women’s careers, calls PIL a bid to ‘instil fear’
articles.summary: The Supreme Court dismissed a Public Interest Litigation (PIL) seeking paid menstrual leave, stating that such policies could negatively impact women's careers. The court said the PIL aimed to 'instil fear' by suggesting women are not on par with men during menstruation. The ruling has implications for workplace policies and gender equality in India.
articles.raw_summary: Dismissing PIL, SC said: 'This is only to create impression in young women that you have some natural issues, and are not at par with males, and you cannot work like them during a particular time.'

articles.title: SC sets record straight for OBC quota—social status, not paycheck must be measure for ‘creamy layer’
articles.summary: The Supreme Court ruled that social status, not just parental income, should determine 'creamy layer' status for OBC reservations. This decision impacts students who were denied benefits based solely on their parents' salaries. The ruling clarifies eligibility for government-backed educational opportunities under affirmative action policies.
articles.raw_summary: Students involved in legal challenges were successful Civil Services Examination candidates whose OBC (non-creamy layer) status was rejected based on parents’ salaries.

articles.title: Of love, loss, medicine & mercy: In passive euthanasia ruling, SC notes ‘true meaning’ of love & dignity

The ruling, based on Harish Rana's case, underscores that abandonment is a greater tragedy than death, setting a precedent for medical and legal practices in India.
articles.raw_summary: Harish Rana’s case revealed a fundamental truth about human existence: ‘The greatest tragedy in life is not death, but abandonment’, the top court said in its landmark judgement.

articles.title: Ola Electric launches #EndICEAge campaign to accelerate EV adoption
articles.summary: Ola Electric has launched the #EndICEAge campaign to promote electric vehicle (EV) adoption in India. The campaign emphasizes the rising costs and inconveniences of petrol dependency, urging riders to switch to EVs. This aligns with India's broader policy push for sustainable transportation and reducing fossil fuel reliance.
articles.raw_summary: The campaign highlights the rising cost and inconvenience of petrol dependency while encouraging riders across the country to make the shift to electric vehicles.

articles.title: Meta ends end-to-end encryption for Instagram DMs: what this means
articles.summary: Meta has ended optional end-to-end encryption for Instagram DMs, reverting chats to unencrypted mode after May 8. This gives Meta access to user content for scanning and moderation, prioritizing safety over privacy. The move follows lawsuits over platform harms and may impact discussions on digital rights and surveillance in India.
articles.raw_summary: Instagram had introduced the optional end-to-end encryption in December 2023. Users had to enable it manually per chat, unlike the default encryption available on WhatsApp or Messenger. After May 8, these chats will revert to the unencrypted mode, giving Meta access to content for scanning and moderation. The move follows Instagram prioritising safety over secrecy, amid lawsuits over platform harms.

articles.title: Indian-origin AI researcher Devendra Chaplot joins Elon Musk's xAI
articles.summary: In the new role, Chaplot will work closely with Musk and the teams at both companies to build superintelligence. Earlier, Chaplot worked in academia, Big Tech, research, and AI startups, with a focus on robotics, embodied AI and LLMs. Part of the founding team at French tech company Mistral AI, Chaplot has contributed to models such as Mistral 7B and Mixtral. He has also worked at Thinking Machines Lab, which works on large-scale training infrastructure and tools for foundation models.

With engineering and business education in India and the US, he previously worked at Apple, Silicon Graphics, and founded a startup. He remains board chair, holding patents and global recognition.

articles.title: Chip firms tap Gujarat govt for free trade zone and shared warehouse
articles.summary: Semiconductor equipment makers, memory storage suppliers, and chip packaging/testing companies have approached the Gujarat government to set up an exclusive free trade zone and shared warehousing infrastructure. This request, if approved, could boost the semiconductor sector in India by providing logistical and regulatory benefits. The move highlights the industry's demand for policy support to enhance manufacturing and trade efficiency.

articles.title: IAMAI urges Karnataka to defer gig worker welfare fee on ecomm firms till specifics are defined
articles.summary: The Karnataka government has mandated ecommerce companies to pay 1% of their commission to fund gig worker welfare under the 2025 law. The Internet and Mobile Association of India (IAMAI) has urged the state to defer the fee until specifics are defined. This affects gig workers' social security and ecommerce operations in Karnataka.
articles.raw_summary: Per the Karnataka Platform-based Gig Workers (Social Security Welfare) Act, 2025, ecommerce operators are required to pay 1% of their commission, subject to a cap, to fund gig workers’ welfare.

articles.title: Induction cooktops sold out on Amazon, quick commerce apps amid LPG cylinder shortage
articles.summary: Induction cooktops are selling out on Amazon, Zepto, Swiggy, and Blinkit due to a shortage of commercial LPG cylinders. Restaurants and cloud kitchens are shutting down or scaling back operations. The supply disruption may lead to policy interventions to stabilize fuel availability.
articles.raw_summary: Induction cooktops are selling rapidly on ecommerce and quick commerce platforms after a shortage of commercial gas cylinders sparked panic buying. Products are now unavailable on Amazon, Zepto, Swiggy and Blinkit in major cities. The LPG supply pause has also forced restaurants and cloud kitchens to shut or scale down operations.

articles.title: China announces anti-dumping duty on certain rubber imports from India, Japan, Canada
articles.summary: China will soon implement anti-dumping duties on halogenated butyl rubber. This action follows an investigation into imports from India, Japan, and Canada. The Chinese Ministry of Commerce found that these products were sold at unfairly low prices. Tariffs of up to 30.1% will affect shipments from Japan and Canada starting March 14.

articles.summary: Consumer inflation for Q4 FY26 is projected at 3 percent, below RBI's estimate. This forecast follows a shift in the CPI base year. articles.title: India’s West Asia trade artery under strain with up to $4 billion in monthly exports at risk
articles.summary: The Gulf war is beginning to disrupt key maritime routes used for India’s exports, putting up to $4 billion of monthly shipments at risk if the situation continues for a month, according to the Federation of Indian Export Organisations (FIEO). Exporters are trying to reroute cargo and explore alternative markets as shipping and air logistics face delays and rising costs.

Thailand's currency depreciation is affecting its rice prices.

articles.summary: The U.S. Trade Representative's office has launched unfair trade practice probes into 60 economies for failing to act on forced labor. articles.summary: In several cases, individuals working in technology and consulting sectors allegedly received part of their compensation in Stablecoins or other cryptocurrencies that were parked in wallets linked with accounts outside India, in low-tax jurisdictions, sources said. The probe also flagged residents investing in overseas crypto exchange-traded funds (ETFs) through offshore brokerage accounts without reporting holdings or gains.

articles.title: Retail inflation climbs to 3.21% in February
articles.summary: Retail inflation in India rose to 3.21% in February, with food and beverage prices contributing significantly to the increase. Core inflation remained steady at 3.4%. Aditi Nayar, chief economist at ICRA, noted that the rise was largely driven by the food and beverages segment. This data could influence future policy decisions by the Reserve Bank of India (RBI) and the government.
articles.raw_summary: Sequentially, retail inflation increased 0.1%, while food inflation fell 0.2%. Core inflation remained steady at 3.4%. "The uptick was almost entirely led by the food and beverages (F B) segment, which accounted for as much as 44 basis points of the 47 bps rise in the headline print between these months," said Aditi Nayar, chief economist at ICRA. One basis point is 0.01 percentage point.

articles.title: Food prices push India's inflation to 3.21% in February
articles.summary: Retail inflation climbed to 3.21% in February, primarily driven by a surge in food and beverage prices. This uptick, the second under the revised CPI series, saw food inflation rise significantly, with personal care and precious metals also contributing to the increase. Future inflation may be impacted by Middle East tensions and rupee depreciation.

articles.title: India's sunflower oil imports halve in February amid West Asia conflict, rising prices
articles.summary: The Solvent Extractors' Association of India (SEA) said average import price for crude sunflower oil rose 17 per cent to USD 1,420 per tonne in February from USD 1,216 a year earlier, while the rupee's 4.2 per cent depreciation over the past year compounded costs for importers and refiners.

articles.title: India begins probe into circumvention of countervailing duty on saccharin imports
articles.summary: India has launched an investigation into claims that saccharin imports from China are being rerouted through Thailand to avoid existing duties. The Directorate General of Trade Remedies is examining if Chinese-origin saccharin is being falsely declared as originating from Thailand. If confirmed, India may extend countervailing duties to imports from Thailand. This probe impacts industries like food, beverage, and pharmaceuticals.

The Directorate General of Trade Remedies is examining if Chinese-origin saccharin is being falsely declared as originating from Thailand. If confirmed, India may extend countervailing duties to imports from Thailand. This probe impacts industries like food, beverage, and pharmaceuticals.

articles.title: India’s February retail inflation quickens to 3.21% before Iran war impact hits
articles.summary: India's retail inflation rose to 3.21% in February, driven by geopolitical tensions impacting global oil supplies. This marks the second reading under a revised CPI framework with a 2024 base year, reflecting updated consumption patterns. While food and fuel inflation figures are pending, economists monitor potential imported inflation risks.

articles.title: Exporters seek priority allocation of LPG and natural gas for manufacturing units
articles.summary: Indian exporters face production challenges due to fuel disruptions. FIEO has requested the government for priority allocation of LPG and natural gas to export-oriented manufacturing units. This is crucial for maintaining production schedules and timely deliveries to international buyers. Ensuring fuel availability will protect existing orders and boost India's reliability as a sourcing destination.

articles.title: Trust, trade and the slow architecture of India-Middle East-Europe Economic Corridor
articles.summary: Launched in 2024, the Indo-Mediterranean Initiative, embodied by the India-Middle East-Europe Economic Corridor (IMEC), is evolving as a strategic, layered process. Despite regional conflicts, IMEC is being built through trade agreements, defense cooperation, and infrastructure partnerships, prioritizing trust and resilience over mere connectivity.

articles.title: US begins new unfair trade probe against overproduction in India, 15 others
articles.summary: The US has launched a trade investigation into excess industrial capacity in 16 countries, including India, under Section 301 of its Trade Act. This probe could lead to new tariffs as the US claims overproduction by trading partners displaces American manufacturing and hinders investment. Evidence suggests significant excess capacity in India's petrochemicals, steel, and solar module sectors.

articles.title: India to release product list for faster PN3 FDI approvals
articles.summary: The amended foreign direct investment (FDI) policy guidelines have allowed proposals for investments from China and other land-border countries to include advanced battery components, rare earth permanent magnets, rare earth processing sectors, whose applications will be processed and decided within 60 days.

articles.title: West Asia conflict could hurt agri input availability: UPL Executive
articles.summary: Geopolitical tensions in West Asia are increasing costs for agricultural inputs like fertilizers, with prices rising 50-80%. While current inventories ensure stable supply for the upcoming kharif season, sustained disruptions could force policy interventions to stabilize prices and ensure food security in India.

While shipping, insurance, and energy costs have climbed, immediate agrochemical availability for the upcoming kharif season remains stable due to pre-built inventories.

articles.title: Iran war impact: Govt mulling diversion of stranded cargo to new markets
articles.summary: India is assessing the diversion of export cargoes stranded by the West Asia crisis to alternative markets. The commerce ministry is seeking details from goods exporters on rerouting options and has also reached out to services exporters to gauge potential risks to trade, including movement restrictions and technology reliance.

articles.title: Centre holds back on fuel exports ban due to logistics, tax hurdles as West Asia conflict rages
articles.summary: India is not banning fuel exports despite global supply issues. Logistical hurdles, contractual obligations with overseas buyers, and tax waivers for export-only refineries are key reasons. Reliance Industries and Nayara Energy are major exporters. China and Thailand have curbed their exports. India aims to avoid adding to global market turbulence. Domestic refiners prefer selling to state retailers for better returns.

articles.title: Private sector optimism rises to decade high on strong demand
articles.summary: With the dawn of a new year on the horizon, Indian firms are brimming with optimism. Consumer demand remains robust, driving companies to invest significantly in innovative projects and R D. Yet, the dark cloud of rising oil prices threatens to dampen this enthusiasm. Nonetheless, this surge of positivity is the strongest we've witnessed in ten years.

articles.title: FDI easing not for Chinese firms; to benefit entities with minority Chinese holding
articles.summary: India has eased foreign investment rules. Overseas firms with up to ten percent Chinese shareholding can now invest automatically. Companies from China and nations sharing land borders with India will get faster approvals for investments in key manufacturing sectors. This aims to boost domestic production and attract foreign capital. The government is streamlining processes for greater certainty for investors.

articles.summary: India's economic growth faces a potential slowdown next financial year due to ongoing West Asian conflict and rising oil prices. Economists warn inflation could also accelerate if tensions persist. The impact hinges on crude oil prices and the conflict's duration. Higher fuel costs may affect consumers, while oil companies might absorb initial burdens. This scenario could limit interest rate cuts.

articles.title: Help farmers shift from paddy to pulses, SC suggests to govt
articles.summary: The Supreme Court has suggested to the Indian government that farmers should be encouraged to shift from paddy cultivation to pulses. This recommendation comes as part of broader discussions on agricultural policy and sustainability. The court's advice highlights the need for policy interventions to address crop diversification and food security.

articles.title: Tata, Parle, M&M canteens feel LPG squeeze; India Inc redesigns menu — less tea, no samosas or dosas
articles.summary: India's industrial sector faces disruption as commercial LPG shortages impact factory operations. Manufacturers report production hurdles, particularly in brazing and paint shops, while canteens are paring back menus. Companies are turning to electric alternatives and even firewood to cope with the 'hand-to-mouth' supply situation, with government intervention underway to prioritize household needs.
articles.raw_summary: India's industrial sector faces disruption as commercial LPG shortages impact factory operations. Manufacturers report production hurdles, particularly in brazing and paint shops, while canteens are paring back menus. Companies are turning to electric alternatives and even firewood to cope with the 'hand-to-mouth' supply situation, with government intervention underway to prioritize household needs.

articles.title: A group of Muslims breaking Ramzan fast attacked by mob in Pune; FIR filed
articles.summary: A mob of around 200 people allegedly attacked 14 Muslims who were breaking their Ramzan fast near Bopdeo Ghat in Pune. The police have registered an FIR and launched an investigation into the incident.

articles.title: NCERT controversy exposes gaps in text-book approval process
articles.summary: The controversy over NCERT textbooks highlights flaws in the approval process for new textbooks aligned with the National Curriculum Framework- School Education (NCF-SE) 2023. The three-stage preparation process is under scrutiny, raising concerns about policy oversight in education.
articles.raw_summary: The preparation of the new textbooks in line with the National Curriculum Framework- School Education (NCF-SE) 2023 follows a three-stage process.

articles.title: Lift ban on CPI(Maoist), free jailed leaders: Devji
articles.summary: CPI (Maoist) leader Devji has urged the Indian government to lift the ban on the party, arguing that it would end armed insurgency and allow for political engagement. The demand highlights tensions between security policies and civil liberties, though no official action has been taken.

articles.title: Increase vigil to curb felling of trees for fuel, says minister
articles.summary: State forest minister Eshwar B Khandre has urged officials to enhance vigilance against illegal tree felling due to rising firewood use amid LPG shortages. The directive aims to prevent deforestation and ensure sustainable fuel supply.
articles.raw_summary: State forest minister Eshwar B Khandre has urged officials to enhance vigilance against illegal tree felling due to rising firewood use amid LPG shortages.

articles.title: Hotels’ wait for LPG set to prolong
articles.summary: Karnataka is prioritizing LPG supply for hospitals and schools over hotels due to shipment delays, leading to a 10-day wait for the hospitality sector. This decision reflects the state's policy to ensure critical services have uninterrupted fuel access amid shortages.
articles.raw_summary: Karnataka hotels may face a 10-day wait for LPG as the state prioritizes supply for hospitals and schools amid shipment delays.

articles.title: IIT Gandhinagar cuts ties with prof amid NCERT textbook row
articles.summary: IIT Gandhinagar ended its association with guest professor Michel Danino after the Supreme Court ordered the institution to dissociate from him and two others involved in drafting a withdrawn NCERT Class 8 textbook that included a section on 'corruption in the judiciary'. The court's directive highlights tensions between academic freedom and judicial oversight in educational content.
articles.raw_summary: IIT Gandhinagar on Friday discontinued its association with its guest professor Michel Danino, two days after the SC ordered to dissociate from him and two others involved in drafting a now-withdrawn NCERT Class 8 textbook which had a section on “corruption in the judiciary”

articles.title: Book controversy reveals gaps in NCERT process
articles.summary: A controversy over a withdrawn Class 8 textbook chapter on judicial corruption has exposed flaws in the NCERT's three-stage approval process for textbooks aligned with the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020. The incident raises concerns about the effectiveness of the policy implementation framework for educational content.
articles.raw_summary: The controversy over the now-withdrawn Class 8 social science textbook with a chapter on judicial corruption has highlighted inadequacies of the three-stage process followed by the National Council of Educational Research and Training (NCERT) to approve new books that align with the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020

articles.title: A decade later, SC rejects Mizo chief council’s claim on Lushai Hills
articles.summary: The Supreme Court rejected a plea by the Mizo Chief Council claiming ownership of land in Mizoram, ending a decade-long legal dispute over land acquired by the state under a 1954 law. The ruling settles the issue of compensation for the acquired land, impacting land rights and tribal governance in the region.
articles.raw_summary: The Supreme Court on Friday rejected a plea by the Mizo Chief Council claiming absolute ownership of land in the erstwhile Lushai Hills (modern-day Mizoram) and seeking enhanced compensation, settling a decade-old legal dispute over land acquired by the State under a 1954 law

articles.title: IndiGo adds ‘fuel charge’ on flight ticket prices as oil spikes amid Iran war
articles.summary: IndiGo has introduced a smaller fuel charge on flight tickets due to the surge in aviation turbine fuel (ATF) prices. The airline says a larger adjustment was needed but opted for a smaller increase. This move comes amid rising oil prices due to geopolitical tensions, which could impact air travel costs and potentially lead to policy discussions on fuel pricing or aviation regulations.
articles.raw_summary: IndiGo says it has opted for a smaller fuel charge despite the surge in ATF prices requiring a “substantial adjustment” to base flight ticket prices.
